page: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Dalsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 AMD processor architecture. The instance is configured with 320.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This server type does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no integrated graphics processing units or hardware accelerators, and it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The hardware profile presents a clear tradeoff optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance offers high compute density. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel processing tasks where memory capacity is not the primary bottleneck, avoiding the premium costs associated with larger memory allocations.

Appropriate workloads for the D160als_v7 include high-performance computing tasks, batch processing, large-scale compilation, and compute-bound microservices. It is also suitable for distributed analytics engines where data is partitioned across many cores with low memory overhead per thread.
description: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine instance from the Dalsv7 family, powered by AMD processors and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 160 dedicated vCPUs and 320.0 GB of system memory, establishing a compute-optimized 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on external Premium Storage capabilities for data persistence. The lack of local disks and the low memory-to-core ratio represent a design tradeoff that maximizes compute density while maintaining qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks. It is designed for workloads such as batch processing, large-scale software compilation, compute-intensive microservices, and distributed parallel computing where memory capacity is not the primary constraint.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 160 dedicated AMD vCPUs and 320 GB of RAM. It is optimized for compute-dense workloads requiring minimal memory per core and no local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 160 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 320 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring one hundred sixty dedicated cores and a balanced two gigabytes of memory per vCPU.
